accepted.title: Adversarial Collaborative Perception in Autonomous Driving
accepted.venue: IEEE International Symposium on Distributed Simulation and Real-Time Applications
accepted.reason: Studies adversarial robustness of multi-agent collaborative perception (MACP) across early/intermediate fusion with evaluation.

accepted.title: CoVeRaP: Cooperative Vehicular Perception through mmWave FMCW Radars
accepted.venue: International Conference on Computer Communications and Networks
accepted.reason: Multi-vehicle cooperative radar perception dataset and middle/late-fusion framework for 3D detection.

accepted.title: Robustifying 3D Perception via Least-Squares Graphs for Multi-Agent Object Tracking
accepted.venue: Annual Conference of the IEEE Industrial Electronics Society
accepted.reason: Multi-vehicle detection fusion and tracking evaluated on V2V4Real for adversarial robustness.

accepted.title: Knowledge-Informed Multi-Agent Trajectory Prediction at Signalized Intersections for Infrastructure-to-Everything
accepted.venue: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
accepted.reason: Infrastructure-to-Everything (I2X) collaborative trajectory prediction where RSUs forecast and transmit to vehicles, evaluated on V2X-Seq.

accepted.title: U2AD: A UAV-Assisted Autonomous Driving Framework for Enhancing Vehicle Risk Perception and Decision-Making Capabilities
accepted.venue: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing
accepted.reason: UAV-to-vehicle cooperative framework extending ego-vehicle perception range and risk perception, evaluated vs V2V/V2I.

accepted.title: TrajAgents: A Multi-Agent Framework for Interpretable and Semantically Consistent V2I Trajectory Prediction
accepted.venue: IEEE/ASME International Conference on Mechatronic and Embedded Systems and Applications
accepted.reason: V2I cooperative trajectory prediction leveraging roadside sensing context, evaluated on V2X-Seq-TFD.

accepted.title: ViTraj: Learning Dual-Side Representations for Vehicle-Infrastructure Cooperative Trajectory Prediction
accepted.reason: Vehicle-infrastructure cooperative trajectory prediction fusing infrastructure-side and vehicle-side trajectories.

accepted.title: MSMA: Multi-agent Trajectory Prediction in Connected and Autonomous Vehicle Environment with Multi-source Data Integration
accepted.reason: CAV fuses own sensors with communicated data from connected vehicles for multi-agent trajectory prediction.

accepted.title: TEA-IHPCR: Temporal Augmented-Invariant Vehicle-Infrastructure Cooperative Heterogeneous Point Cloud Registration
accepted.venue: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
accepted.reason: Vehicle-infrastructure cooperative heterogeneous LiDAR point cloud registration enabling cooperative perception.

accepted.title: Mobility-Aware Sensing Data Orchestration for Communication-Efficient Cooperative Perception
accepted.venue: International Conference on Computing, Networking and Communications
accepted.reason: Sensing-data sharing/selection framework for CAV cooperative perception, evaluated on real driving datasets for perception quality.

accepted.title: PosiFusion: A Vehicle-to-Everything Cooperative Perception Framework with Positional Prior Fusion
accepted.venue: ISPRS Annals of the Photogrammetry, Remote Sensing and Spatial Information Sciences
accepted.reason: V2X collaborative perception with positional prior fusion, evaluated on OPV2V and V2XSet.

accepted.title: V2X Fusion Communication Framework Based on VANETS Collaborative Autonomous Driving
accepted.reason: Communication-interruption-aware cooperative perception model fusing multi-agent LiDAR/radar/camera data with knowledge distillation.

accepted.title: Vehicle-to-Infrastructure Multi-Sensor Fusion (V2I-MSF) With Reinforcement Learning Framework for Enhancing Autonomous Vehicle Perception
accepted.reason: V2I framework fusing vehicle OBU LiDAR/camera with roadside sensor data to enhance perception, evaluated in simulation.

accepted.title: Spatio-Temporal Interaction Aware Cooperative Perception for Networked Vehicles
accepted.reason: V2V cooperative perception with interaction-aware selective sensor fusion among connected vehicles, evaluated on CARLA.

accepted.title: Supply-Demand-Driven Information Selection Algorithm for Efficient V2X Perception
accepted.venue: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Internet of Things and Cloud Computing Technology (AIoTC)
accepted.reason: Multi-agent V2X collaborative perception with supply-demand feature selection, evaluated on DAIR-V2X vs Where2comm.

accepted.title: InterCoop: Spatio-Temporal Interaction Aware Cooperative Perception for Networked Vehicles
accepted.venue: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation
accepted.reason: V2V cooperative perception with interaction-score-based selective fusion across connected vehicles, evaluated on CARLA.
